title: NULL name not rejected despite _Nonnull annotation
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/senml/senml.c
Lines: 83-107, 110-137, 139-163

CATEGORY: API MISUSE / CONTRACT VIOLATION

The header declares name parameter as _Nonnull (line 121-122 in senml.h):
  int senml_add_float(struct senml_pack *_Nonnull pack,
                      const char *_Nonnull name, ...)

However, the implementation does not enforce this. The validate_name() function at line 50-53 calls string_too_long() which returns:
  return str != NULL && strnlen(str, max_len + 1) > max_len;

If name is NULL, this returns false (validation passes). The NULL is then stored in the record and later in encode_record() at line 209, it silently skips encoding the name field.

IMPACT:
- API contract violation: _Nonnull promises non-NULL but NULL passes through
- Silent data loss: name field disappears without error
- RFC 8428 allows empty name only when base_name is set, but NULL != empty

FIX: Add explicit NULL check before validate_name() or modify validate_name() to reject NULL when the parameter is documented as required.
